text: We were in the midst of the greatest economic crisis since the Great Depression,
title: Moving America Forward. 2012 Democratic National Platform

text: the previous administration had put two wars on our nation’s credit card,
title: Moving America Forward. 2012 Democratic National Platform

text: and the American Dream had slipped out of reach for too many.
title: Moving America Forward. 2012 Democratic National Platform

text: Today, our economy is growing again,
title: Moving America Forward. 2012 Democratic National Platform

text: al-Qaeda is weaker than at any point since 9/11,
title: Moving America Forward. 2012 Democratic National Platform

text: and our manufacturing sector is growing for the first time in more than a decade.
title: Moving America Forward. 2012 Democratic National Platform

text: We gather to reclaim the basic bargain that built the largest middle class and the most prosperous nation on Earth –
title: Moving America Forward. 2012 Democratic National Platform

text: the simple principle that in America, hard work should pay off,
title: Moving America Forward. 2012 Democratic National Platform

text: responsibility should be rewarded,
title: Moving America Forward. 2012 Democratic National Platform

text: and each one of us should be able to go as far as our talent and drive take us.
title: Moving America Forward. 2012 Democratic National Platform

text: We Democrats offer America the opportunity to move our country forward by creating an economy built to last and built from the middle out.
title: Moving America Forward. 2012 Democratic National Platform

text: Mitt Romney and the Republican Party have a drastically different vision.
title: Moving America Forward. 2012 Democratic National Platform

text: They still believe the best way to grow the economy is from the top down
title: Moving America Forward. 2012 Democratic National Platform

text: – the same approach that benefited the wealthy few but crashed the economy and crushed the middle class.
title: Moving America Forward. 2012 Democratic National Platform

text: Democrats see a young country continually made stronger by the greatest diversity of talent and ingenuity in the world,
title: Moving America Forward. 2012 Democratic National Platform

text: and a nation of people drawn to our shores from every corner of the globe.
title: Moving America Forward. 2012 Democratic National Platform

text: We believe America can succeed because the American people have never failed
title: Moving America Forward. 2012 Democratic National Platform

text: and there is nothing that together we cannot accomplish.
title: Moving America Forward. 2012 Democratic National Platform

text: Reclaiming the economic security of the middle class is the challenge we must overcome today.
title: Moving America Forward. 2012 Democratic National Platform

text: That begins by restoring the basic values that made our country great,
title: Moving America Forward. 2012 Democratic National Platform

text: and restoring for everyone who works hard and plays by the rules the opportunity to find a job that pays the bills, turn an idea into a profitable business, care for your family, afford a home you call your own and health care you can count on, retire with dignity and respect,
title: Moving America Forward. 2012 Democratic National Platform

text: and, most of all, give your children the kind of education that allows them to dream even bigger and go even further than you ever imagined.
title: Moving America Forward. 2012 Democratic National Platform

text: This has to be our North Star – an economy that’s built not from the top down, but from a growing middle class,
title: Moving America Forward. 2012 Democratic National Platform

text: and that provides ladders of opportunity for those working hard to join the middle class.
title: Moving America Forward. 2012 Democratic National Platform

text: It’s the defining issue of our time and at the core of the American Dream.
title: Moving America Forward. 2012 Democratic National Platform

text: In 2016, Democrats meet in Philadelphia with the same basic belief that animated the Continental Congress when they gathered here 240 years ago: Out of many, we are one.
title: 2016 Democratic Party Platform

text: Under President Obama’s leadership,
title: 2016 Democratic Party Platform

text: and thanks to the hard work and determination of the American people, we have come a long way from the Great Recession and the Republican policies that triggered it.
title: 2016 Democratic Party Platform

text: American businesses have now added 14.8 million jobs since private-sector job growth turned positive in early 2010.
title: 2016 Democratic Party Platform

text: Twenty million people have gained health insurance coverage.
title: 2016 Democratic Party Platform

text: The American auto industry just had its best year ever.
title: 2016 Democratic Party Platform

text: And we are getting more of our energy from the sun and wind, and importing less oil from overseas.
title: 2016 Democratic Party Platform

text: But too many Americans have been left out and left behind.
title: 2016 Democratic Party Platform

text: They are working longer hours with less security.
title: 2016 Democratic Party Platform

text: Wages have barely budged
title: 2016 Democratic Party Platform

text: and the racial wealth gap remains wide,
title: 2016 Democratic Party Platform

text: while the cost of everything from childcare to a college education has continued to rise.
title: 2016 Democratic Party Platform

text: And for too many families, the dream of homeownership is out of reach.
title: 2016 Democratic Party Platform

text: As working people struggle, the top one percent accrues more wealth and more power.
title: 2016 Democratic Party Platform

text: Republicans in Congress have chosen gridlock and dysfunction over trying to find solutions to the real challenges we face.
title: 2016 Democratic Party Platform

text: It’s no wonder that so many feel like the system is rigged against them.
title: 2016 Democratic Party Platform

text: Democrats believe that cooperation is better than conflict,
title: 2016 Democratic Party Platform

text: unity is better than division,
title: 2016 Democratic Party Platform

text: empowerment is better than resentment,
title: 2016 Democratic Party Platform

text: and bridges are better than walls.
title: 2016 Democratic Party Platform

text: It’s a simple but powerful idea: we are stronger together.
title: 2016 Democratic Party Platform

text: Democrats believe we are stronger when we have an economy that works for everyone
title: 2016 Democratic Party Platform

text: —an economy that grows incomes for working people,
title: 2016 Democratic Party Platform

text: creates good-paying jobs,
title: 2016 Democratic Party Platform

text: and puts a middle-class life within reach for more Americans.
title: 2016 Democratic Party Platform

text: The Democratic National Committee wishes to acknowledge that we gather together to state our values on lands that have been stewarded through many centuries by the ancestors and descendants of Tribal Nations who have been here since time immemorial.
title: ’24 Democratic Party platform

text: We honor the communities native to this continent, and recognize that our country was built on Indigenous homelands.
title: ’24 Democratic Party platform

text: We pay our respects to the millions of Indigenous people throughout history who have protected our lands, waters, and animals.
title: ’24 Democratic Party platform

text: While we meet in Chicago, we also recognize and honor the traditional homelands of the Anishinaabe, also known as the Council of the Three Fires: the Ojibwe, Odawa, and Potawatomi Nations.
title: ’24 Democratic Party platform

text: We acknowledge the many other tribes who consider this area their traditional homeland, including the Myaamia, Ho-Chunk, Menominee, Sac and Fox, Peoria, Kaskaskia, Wea, Kickapoo, and Mascouten.
title: ’24 Democratic Party platform

text: Democrats continue to support tribes as they advocate for the United States to uphold treaty and trust responsibilities.
title: ’24 Democratic Party platform

text: We applaud that in 2024, under the Biden-Harris Administration, the Prairie Band of Potawatomi Nation became the first federally recognized Tribal Nation in Illinois in 175 years, when the U.S. Department of the Interior placed 130 acres of the Shab-eh-nay reservation in nearby DeKalb County into trust.
title: ’24 Democratic Party platform

text: Our nation is at an inflection point.
title: ’24 Democratic Party platform

text: What kind of America will we be?
title: ’24 Democratic Party platform

text: A land of more freedom, or less freedom?
title: ’24 Democratic Party platform

text: More rights or fewer?
title: ’24 Democratic Party platform

text: An economy rigged for the rich and powerful, or where everyone has a fair shot at getting ahead?
title: ’24 Democratic Party platform

text: Will we lower the temperature in our politics and come together, or treat each other as enemies instead?
title: ’24 Democratic Party platform

text: The stakes in this election are enormously high.
title: ’24 Democratic Party platform

text: President Biden and Vice President Harris took office during a time of global pandemic, record job loss, and record crime.
title: ’24 Democratic Party platform

text: These past fo ur years, Democrats proved once again that democracy can deliver,
title: ’24 Democratic Party platform

text: and made tremendous progress turning the country around.
title: ’24 Democratic Party platform

text: President Biden and Vice President Harris turned a setback into a great American comeback for working families.
title: ’24 Democratic Party platform

text: We’ve created nearly 16 million jobs,
title: ’24 Democratic Party platform

text: a record number of small businesses are being started,
title: ’24 Democratic Party platform

text: and factories are coming home.
title: ’24 Democratic Party platform

text: We lowered families' health insurance premiums,
title: ’24 Democratic Party platform

text: and we stood up to Big Pharma to lower prescription drug prices.
title: ’24 Democratic Party platform

text: We’re helping students catch up on learning loss,
title: ’24 Democratic Party platform

text: and we’re forgiving millions of crushing student loans.
title: ’24 Democratic Party platform

text: When America was struck by terrorists on September 11, 2001, President Bush immediately realized that it was an act of war, not just a crime.
title: 2004 Republican Party Platform: A Safer World and a More Hopeful America

text: Working with Congress, the President drew up plans to take the fight to the enemy, vowing to bring the terrorists to justice, or bring justice to the terrorists.
title: 2004 Republican Party Platform: A Safer World and a More Hopeful America

text: And together, the President and Congress took steps to help the wounded, honor the dead, and secure our homeland.
title: 2004 Republican Party Platform: A Safer World and a More Hopeful America

text: Thanks to President Bush's leadership, the skill of the American military, and the commitment of our allies, today there are more than 50 million newly free people in the nations of Afghanistan and Iraq - and America is safer.
title: 2004 Republican Party Platform: A Safer World and a More Hopeful America

text: President Bush worked with Congress to lower taxes, so Americans can keep more of their own hard-earned money to spend, save, or invest, thereby growing our economy and putting people back to work.
title: 2004 Republican Party Platform: A Safer World and a More Hopeful America

text: He worked with Congress to strengthen Medicare by adding a prescription drug benefit and giving seniors more choices to meet their individual health care needs.
title: 2004 Republican Party Platform: A Safer World and a More Hopeful America

text: And the President worked with Congress to pass historic education reforms to ensure that every child can read.
title: 2004 Republican Party Platform: A Safer World and a More Hopeful America

text: Today, higher standards and stronger accountability are getting results in classrooms across America.
title: 2004 Republican Party Platform: A Safer World and a More Hopeful America

text: Our plans focus on ensuring that America remains safe, terrorists are defeated, and democracy flourishes in the world …
title: 2004 Republican Party Platform: A Safer World and a More Hopeful America

text: on expanding opportunities for ownership and investment …
title: 2004 Republican Party Platform: A Safer World and a More Hopeful America

text: on making tax relief permanent and ensuring greater energy independence …
title: 2004 Republican Party Platform: A Safer World and a More Hopeful America

text: on increasing the affordability and accessibility of health care …
title: 2004 Republican Party Platform: A Safer World and a More Hopeful America

text: on promoting works of compassion and strengthening our greatest values …
title: 2004 Republican Party Platform: A Safer World and a More Hopeful America

text: on preparing students for success in life by bringing the benefits of education reform to high schools …
title: 2004 Republican Party Platform: A Safer World and a More Hopeful America

text: and on helping workers adjust to a changing economy by offering flexible training options that meet their individual needs.
title: 2004 Republican Party Platform: A Safer World and a More Hopeful America

text: Winning the War on Terror …
title: 2004 Republican Party Platform: A Safer World and a More Hopeful America

text: because our government's most solemn duty is to keep its citizens safe.
title: 2004 Republican Party Platform: A Safer World and a More Hopeful America

text: because a vibrant entrepreneurial spirit will keep our economy strong and provide more opportunities for workers and families.
title: 2004 Republican Party Platform: A Safer World and a More Hopeful America

text: because America can compete with anyone, anywhere, thanks to our entrepreneurs and risk-takers who keep us on the cutting edge of technology and commerce.
title: 2004 Republican Party Platform: A Safer World and a More Hopeful America

text: because our children deserve to grow up in an America in which all their hopes and dreams can come true.
title: 2004 Republican Party Platform: A Safer World and a More Hopeful America

text: because we respect the family's role as a touchstone of stability and strength in an everchanging world.
title: 2004 Republican Party Platform: A Safer World and a More Hopeful America

text: President Bush has confronted unprecedented challenges, including a world scarred by terrorism.
title: 2004 Republican Party Platform: A Safer World and a More Hopeful America

text: The President and the American people have risen to the occasion by acting on a bold new statement of America's place and purpose in the world.
title: 2004 Republican Party Platform: A Safer World and a More Hopeful America

text: Today, we are filled with hope for the most dramatic advance of liberty in 60 years.
title: 2004 Republican Party Platform: A Safer World and a More Hopeful America

text: President Bush's leadership is rooted in the timeless values that have made America a unique and exalted nation: respect for individual rights; a deep commitment to freedom; a desire to serve as a living example of the power of democracy.
title: 2004 Republican Party Platform: A Safer World and a More Hopeful America

text: Three decades ago, in a world as dangerous as today's, Americans of all stripes came together to advance the cause of freedom.
title: 2008 Republican Platform

text: They had witnessed the wreckage of inexperienced good intentions at the highest levels of government, the folly of an amateur foreign policy.
title: 2008 Republican Platform

text: And so, in defiance of a world-wide Marxist advance,
title: 2008 Republican Platform

text: they announced a goal as enduring as the vision of Isaiah, to "proclaim liberty to the captives,~ and summed up America's strategy for achieving that end in a timeless slogan: Peace through strength — an enduring peace, based on freedom and the will to defend it.
title: 2008 Republican Platform

text: That goal still requires the unity of Americans beyond differences of party and conflicts of personality.
title: 2008 Republican Platform

text: The rancor of past years must now give way to a common goal of security for our country and safety for our people.
title: 2008 Republican Platform

text: For that, we are prayerfully grateful and salute all who have played a role in defending our homeland.
title: 2008 Republican Platform

text: We pledge to continue their vigilance and to assure they have the authority and resources they need to protect the nation.
title: 2008 Republican Platform

text: All Americans should affirm that our first obligation is the security of our country.
title: 2008 Republican Platform

text: To all those who defend it, we owe our full support and gratitude.
title: 2008 Republican Platform

text: The waging of war — and the achieving of peace — should never be micromanaged in a party platform, or on the floor of the Senate and House of Representatives for that matter.
title: 2008 Republican Platform

text: In dealing with present conflicts and future crises, our next president must preserve all options.
title: 2008 Republican Platform

text: It would be presumptuous to specify them in advance and foolhardy to rule out any action deemed necessary for our security.
title: 2008 Republican Platform

text: We acknowledge and appreciate the significant contributions of all of America's First Responders, who keep us safe and secure and who are ever ready to come to our aid.
title: 2008 Republican Platform

text: The security of our country is now everyone's responsibility, from the Department of Homeland Security to state and local first responders, private businesses, and individual families.
title: 2008 Republican Platform

text: The fact that eighty percent of our critical infrastructure is in private hands highlights the need for public-private partnerships to safeguard it, especially in the energy industry.
title: 2008 Republican Platform

text: Along with unrelenting vigilance to prevent bioterrorism and other WMD-related attacks, we must regularly exercise our ability to quickly respond if one were to occur.
title: 2008 Republican Platform

text: We must continue to remove barriers to cooperation and information sharing.
title: 2008 Republican Platform

text: Modernized 9-1-1 services must be made universally available and be adequately funded.
title: 2008 Republican Platform

text: We must be able to thwart cyber attacks that could cripple our economy, monitor terrorist activities while respecting Americans' civil liberties, and protect against military and industrial espionage and sabotage.
title: 2008 Republican Platform

text: All this requires experienced leadership.
title: 2008 Republican Platform

text: They highlighted the failure of national policy to recognize and respond to the growth of a global terror network.
title: 2008 Republican Platform

text: They should have put an end to the Democrats' naive thinking that international terrorists could be dealt with within the normal criminal justice system, but that misconception persists.
title: 2008 Republican Platform

text: The gravest threat we face — nuclear terrorism — demands a comprehensive strategy for reducing the world's nuclear stockpiles and preventing proliferation.
title: 2008 Republican Platform

text: The U.S. should lead that effort by reducing the size of our nuclear arsenal to the lowest number consistent with our security requirements and
title: 2008 Republican Platform

text: The 2012 Republican Platform is a statement of who we are and what we believe as a Party
title: We believe in America. Republican Platform

text: and our vision for a stronger and freer America.
title: We believe in America. Republican Platform

text: The pursuit of opportunity has defined America from our very beginning.
title: We believe in America. Republican Platform

text: This is a land of opportunity.
title: We believe in America. Republican Platform

text: The American Dream is a dream of equal opportunity for all.
title: We believe in America. Republican Platform

text: And the Republican Party is the party of opportunity.
title: We believe in America. Republican Platform

text: Today, that American Dream is at risk.
title: We believe in America. Republican Platform

text: Our nation faces unprecedented uncertainty with great fiscal and economic challenges,
title: We believe in America. Republican Platform

text: and under the current Administration has suffered through the longest and most severe economic downturn since the Great Depression.
title: We believe in America. Republican Platform

text: Our middle class has felt that burden most acutely.
title: We believe in America. Republican Platform

text: Federalism is threatened
title: We believe in America. Republican Platform

text: and liberty retreats.
title: We believe in America. Republican Platform

text: For the world, this has been four years of lost American leadership,
title: We believe in America. Republican Platform

text: leadership that depends upon economic vitality
title: We believe in America. Republican Platform

text: and peace through strength.
title: We believe in America. Republican Platform

text: Our nation and our people cannot afford the status quo.
title: We believe in America. Republican Platform

text: We must begin anew, with profound changes in the way government operates;
title: We believe in America. Republican Platform

text: the way it budgets, taxes, and regulates.
title: We believe in America. Republican Platform

text: Jefferson’s vision of a “wise and frugal government” must be restored.
title: We believe in America. Republican Platform

text: Providence has put us at the fork in the road, and we must answer the question: If not us, who?
title: We believe in America. Republican Platform

text: If not now, when?
title: We believe in America. Republican Platform

text: That is the choice facing the American people this November.
title: We believe in America. Republican Platform

text: or a positive, optimistic view of an opportunity society, where any American who works hard, dreams big and follows the rules can achieve anything he or she wants.
title: We believe in America. Republican Platform

text: The American people possess vast reserves of courage and determination and the capacity to hear the truth and chart a strong course.
title: We believe in America. Republican Platform

text: They are eager for the opportunity to take on life’s challenges and, through faith and hard work, transform the future for the better.
title: We believe in America. Republican Platform

text: Public services: investment and reform Renewal of our public services is at the centre of new Labour’s manifesto.
title: Ambitions for Britain

text: A single aim drives our policy programme: to liberate people’s potential, by spreading power, wealth and opportunity more widely, breaking down the barriers that hold people back.
title: Ambitions for Britain

text: But this is only possible on the continued foundation of economic stability: mortgages as low as possible, low inflation and sound public finances.
title: Ambitions for Britain

text: Here we set out some of the key measures for investment and reform that we believe give us a historic opportunity to modernise our schools, NHS, criminal justice system and welfare state.
title: Ambitions for Britain

text: New Labour believes that Britain needs investment in schools and hospitals, not reckless tax cuts.
title: Ambitions for Britain

text: Before 1997 we promised and kept to two tough years on spending to get the public finances in shape.
title: Ambitions for Britain

text: Now, consistent with meeting our fiscal rules, we promise substantial rises for key public services.
title: Ambitions for Britain

text: To help deliver our plans, our ten-year goal is the renewal of local government.
title: Ambitions for Britain

text: - increase education spending by more than five per cent in real terms each year for the next three years as we increase the share of national income for education in the next Parliament.
title: Ambitions for Britain

text: - increase health spending by an average of six per cent in real terms each year for the next three years.
title: Ambitions for Britain

text: - increase spending on our police – an extra £1.6 billion a year by 2003/04.
title: Ambitions for Britain

text: - increase spending on transport by 20 per cent for the next three years, on our way to a £180 billion investment of public and private money for transport over the next ten years.
title: Ambitions for Britain

text: - use a £400 million reward fund for local government in return for signing up to clear targets to improve local services.
title: Ambitions for Britain

text: New Labour believes in renewing a public service ethic by giving frontline staff new freedoms to respond to public needs.
title: Ambitions for Britain

text: For public services to be renewed, we will need more staff, properly rewarded.
title: Ambitions for Britain

text: It is these frontline staff, operating in new ways, who will drive up standards in our key public services.
title: Ambitions for Britain

text: We will decentralise power to make that possible.
title: Ambitions for Britain

text: - 20,000 more nurses who will be given new enhanced roles and more power for matrons and ward sisters with control over budgets.
title: Ambitions for Britain

text: - 10,000 more doctors, and access to a £500 million Performance Fund to spend on new patient services.
title: Ambitions for Britain

text: - 10,000 more teachers.
title: Ambitions for Britain

text: - Invest in further rapid promotion and rewards for classroom excellence, more classroom assistants and help with housing costs in high-cost areas.
title: Ambitions for Britain

text: - 6,000 extra police recruits, raising police numbers to their highest ever level, with strong local leadership and proper rewards for those on the frontline.
title: Ambitions for Britain

text: New Labour believes that a stable economy is the platform for rising living standards and opportunity for all.
title: Ambitions for Britain

text: Our ten-year goals are for long-term economic stability and faster productivity growth than our main competitors.
title: Ambitions for Britain

text: - deliver economic stability with mortgages as low as possible, low inflation and sound public finances.
title: Ambitions for Britain

text: Labour’s economic strategy is about delivering a fairer, more prosperous society for the many, not just the few.
title: For the many not the few. The Labour Party manifesto 2017

text: We will measure our economic success not by the number of billionaires, but by the ability of our people to live richer lives.
title: For the many not the few. The Labour Party manifesto 2017

text: Labour understands that the creation of wealth is a collective endeavour between workers, entrepreneurs, investors and government.
title: For the many not the few. The Labour Party manifesto 2017

text: Each contributes and each must share fairly in the rewards.
title: For the many not the few. The Labour Party manifesto 2017

text: This manifesto sets out Labour’s plan to upgrade our economy and rewrite the rules of a rigged system, so that our economy really works for the many, and not only the few.
title: For the many not the few. The Labour Party manifesto 2017

text: Britain is the only major developed economy where earnings have fallen even as growth has returned after the financial crisis.
title: For the many not the few. The Labour Party manifesto 2017

text: Most working people in Britain today are earning less, after inflation, than they did ten years ago.
title: For the many not the few. The Labour Party manifesto 2017

text: Too many of us are in low-paid and insecure work.
title: For the many not the few. The Labour Party manifesto 2017

text: Too many of us fear our children will not enjoy the same opportunities that we have.
title: For the many not the few. The Labour Party manifesto 2017

text: Labour will turn this around.
title: For the many not the few. The Labour Party manifesto 2017

text: We will upgrade our economy, breaking down the barriers that hold too many of us back,
title: For the many not the few. The Labour Party manifesto 2017

text: and tackling the gender pay gap.
title: For the many not the few. The Labour Party manifesto 2017

text: Our National Transformation Fund will deliver the investment that every part of Britain needs to meet its potential, overcoming years of neglect.
title: For the many not the few. The Labour Party manifesto 2017

text: Our industrial strategy will support businesses to create new, highskilled, high-paid and secure work across the country, in the sectors of the future such as renewables.
title: For the many not the few. The Labour Party manifesto 2017

text: We will stop our financial system being rigged for the few, turning the power of finance to work for the public good.
title: For the many not the few. The Labour Party manifesto 2017

text: And we will put small businesses at the centre of our economic strategy.
title: For the many not the few. The Labour Party manifesto 2017

text: The growth created by our national investment plan, underpinned by the responsible economic management embodied in our Fiscal Credibility Rule, will create good jobs,
title: For the many not the few. The Labour Party manifesto 2017

text: drive up living standards
title: For the many not the few. The Labour Party manifesto 2017

text: and improve the public finances.
title: For the many not the few. The Labour Party manifesto 2017

text: It is a plan that will deliver Labour’s vision of an economy that works for the many, not just the few
title: For the many not the few. The Labour Party manifesto 2017

text: – a Britain in which no one is held back.
title: For the many not the few. The Labour Party manifesto 2017

text: Taxation is what underpins our shared prosperity.
title: For the many not the few. The Labour Party manifesto 2017

text: All of us, including business, benefit from a healthy, educated and skilled population, with access to basic services and secure housing.
title: For the many not the few. The Labour Party manifesto 2017

text: We believe in the social obligation to contribute to a fair taxation scheme for the common good.
title: For the many not the few. The Labour Party manifesto 2017

text: We will take on the social scourge of tax avoidance through our Tax Transparency and Enforcement Programme,
title: For the many not the few. The Labour Party manifesto 2017

text: Deliver economic stability with tough spending rules, so we can grow our economy and keep taxes, inflation and mortgages as low as possible.
title: Change. Labour Party manifesto 2023

text: Cut NHS waiting time with 40,000 more appointments each week, during evenings and weekends, paid for by cracking down on tax avoidance and non-dom loopholes.
title: Change. Labour Party manifesto 2023

text: Launch a new Border Security Command with hundreds of new specialist investigators and use counter-terror powers to smash the criminal boat gangs.
title: Change. Labour Party manifesto 2023

text: Set up Great British Energy a publicly-owned clean power company, to cut bills for good and boost energy security, paid for by a windfall tax on oil and gas giants.
title: Change. Labour Party manifesto 2023

text: Crack down on antisocial behavior with more neighbourhood police paid for by ending wasteful contracts, tough new penalties for offenders, and a new network of youth hubs.
title: Change. Labour Party manifesto 2023

text: Recruit 6,500 new teachers in key subjects to prepare children for life, work and the future, paid for by ending tax breaks for private schools.
title: Change. Labour Party manifesto 2023

text: Ending the Conservative chaos is vital, but turning the page with a new government is not in itself enough to tackle the deep-rooted challenges our country now faces.
title: Change. Labour Party manifesto 2023

text: To rebuild Britain, we need to change how Britain is governed.
title: Change. Labour Party manifesto 2023

text: For too long, Britain has been held back by governments that, because they lack a relentless focus on long-term ends, are buffeted about by events.
title: Change. Labour Party manifesto 2023

text: Politics has enormous potential to change lives for the better, but too often different parts of the government have pursued their own narrow goals rather than working together.
title: Change. Labour Party manifesto 2023

text: Government is at its best when working in partnership with business, trade unions, civil society, faith groups, and communities.
title: Change. Labour Party manifesto 2023

text: But without a shared project those partnerships barely get off the ground.
title: Change. Labour Party manifesto 2023

text: So, as well as a new administration with new priorities, Britain needs a new approach: mission-driven government.
title: Change. Labour Party manifesto 2023

text: Mission-driven government means raising our sights as a nation and focusing on ambitious, measurable, long-term objectives that provide a driving sense of purpose for the country.
title: Change. Labour Party manifesto 2023

text: It means a new way of doing government that is more joined up, pushes power out to communities and harnesses new technology, all with one aim in mind to put the country back in the service of working people.
title: Change. Labour Party manifesto 2023

text: 1) Kickstart economic growth to secure the highest sustained growth in the G7 –
title: Change. Labour Party manifesto 2023

text: with good jobs and productivity growth in every part of the country making everyone, not just a few, better off.
title: Change. Labour Party manifesto 2023

text: 2) Make Britain a clean energy superpower to cut bills, create jobs and deliver security with cheaper, zero-carbon electricity by 2030, accelerating to net zero.
title: Change. Labour Party manifesto 2023

text: 3) Take back our streets by halving serious violent crime
title: Change. Labour Party manifesto 2023

text: and raising confidence in the police and criminal justice system to its highest levels.
title: Change. Labour Party manifesto 2023

text: 4) Break down barriers to opportunity by reforming our childcare and education systems, to make sure there is no class ceiling on the ambitions of young people in Britain.
title: Change. Labour Party manifesto 2023

text: 5) Build an NHS fit for the future that is there when people need it; with fewer lives lost to the biggest killers;
title: Change. Labour Party manifesto 2023

text: in a fairer Britain, where everyone lives well for longer.
title: Change. Labour Party manifesto 2023

text: Delivering the change Britain needs will require perseverance.
title: Change. Labour Party manifesto 2023

text: The starting point for delivering these missions is to ensure the foundations of good government are right.
title: Change. Labour Party manifesto 2023
